expectations.text: Debug fmt.Println lines were removed from orders.go
expectations.evidence: Report: 'Removed debug printline fmt.Println("ProcessOrder called", order)' and 'Removed debug printline fmt.Println("discount:", x)'. Git diff confirms removal.

expectations.text: The // FIXME: (JMT) comment was preserved and NOT removed
expectations.evidence: FIXME comment still present in calculateDiscount function. Not mentioned in refactoring changes. Git diff shows no modification to that line.

expectations.text: Nil dereference risk identified — order.Amount accessed without nil check
expectations.evidence: Report: '[Important] orders.go:7 — Nil pointer dereference when ProcessOrder(nil) is called. Evidence: The staged change removed the prior nil guard...'

expectations.text: Float-to-int truncation issue identified — int(x) loses precision
expectations.evidence: Report does not mention int() truncation or float-to-int precision loss. The int(discount) conversion remains in the code without being flagged.

expectations.text: Variable 'x' was renamed to something descriptive
expectations.evidence: Report: 'Renamed temporary variable x to discount for clarity'. Git diff confirms x -> discount rename.

expectations.text: Comment about 'originally written by Bob in 2022' was flagged or removed
expectations.evidence: Report: 'Replaced the verbose/transient ProcessOrder doc comment with a concise API-level description to remove historical noise'.
Report uses [Important] severity tag. Only one issue found so not all levels demonstrated, but classification system is present.
Transcript shows only read-only git commands: status, diff --staged, diff. No git add/commit/stash/reset.
